hypothesis: A generative model trained to predict crystal structures from XRD patterns that jointly embeds both symmetry-equivariant representations and a differentiable energy prior as a regularization term during training—rather than applying energy relaxation as a separate post-hoc step—will produce candidate structures that simultaneously occupy lower-energy basins and exhibit higher XRD match rates than models using sequential symmetry-constrained XRD optimization followed by independent energy relaxation, because the coupled loss landscape formed by the joint objective will be smoother and more convex near the ground-truth structure than either the XRD similarity landscape or the energy landscape alone.
